summary: Soundboard recording of exceptional clarity captures a fire‑filled 1977 show highlighted by the legendary Stephen > Iko > NFA segue, scorching Bertha and Row Jimmy, plus killer Jack‑A‑Roe and Brown‑Eyed Women.
review: The gd1977‑05‑15.sbd.bertha recording is dialed‑in – a crisp soundboard mix that lets every instrument shine.  Fans rave about the seamless Stephen > Iko > NFA transition, the powerhouse Bertha opener and the soulful Row Jimmy, while Jerry’s lead work, Phil’s bass counterpoint, and the keyboard crew earn special praise.  Overall the performance is described as one of the strongest from the “earth‑trembling” ’77 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: All six detailed reviews are five‑star, overwhelmingly positive about both musical performance and audio fidelity; the weighting favours show quality (70%) which is described as superb.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently praised as ‘dialed‑in’, ‘killer sound’, and ‘adequate recording’ after remastering; no complaints of drop‑outs, muddiness or audience bleed.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > Iko > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight tight transitions (especially the Stephen > Iko > NFA sequence) and a well‑balanced set that moves from high‑octane openers to fluid second‑set segues like Estimated > Eyes, keeping the energy steady throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for ‘great guitar work’, strong lead vocals and fiery jams (e.g., on Bertha, Jack‑A‑Roe).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a ‘counterpoint bass line’ that drives songs like Lightning>Supplication and for powerful vocal moments; described as being in the “Phil Zone”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for solid vocals and interplay with Donna Jean on several numbers (e.g., Bertha, Jack‑A‑Roe).
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s and Brent’s contributions are praised; keyboards add depth to jams and vocal harmonies.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as ‘big drums’, powerful on tracks like Samson & Delilah and the Estimated > Eyes opener.
